entry.title: 222. Count Complete Tree Nodes
entry.content:
LeetCode

Approach 1: Linear Time O(N)

Approach 2: Binary search: {binary search in binary search O(d^2)=O(log^2 N)}

entry.title: 221. Maximal Square
entry.content:
LeetCode

DP: dp(i,j)=min(dp(i−1,j),dp(i−1,j−1),dp(i,j−1))+1

entry.title: 217. Contains Duplicate, 219. Contains Duplicate II, 220. Contains Duplicate III
entry.content:
LeetCode

LeetCode

Approach #1 (Naive Linear Search): Time complexity : O(nmin(k,n))

Approach #2 (Binary Search Tree): Time complexity : O(nlog(min(k,n)))

Approach #3 (Hash Table)

LeetCode

Approach #1 (Naive Linear Search) [Time Limit Exceeded]

Approach #2 (Binary Search Tree) [Accepted]

Approach #3 (Buckets) [Accepted]

entry.title: 215. Kth Largest Element in an Array
entry.content:
LeetCode

Quickselect: O(N)

Java “Arrays.sort()” API (internal implementation: Dual-pivot Quicksort ): O(NlogN)

MergeSort: O(NlogN)

entry.title: 201. Bitwise AND of Numbers Range
entry.content:
https://leetcode.com/problems/bitwise-and-of-numbers-range/

intuitive solutions: exceed the time limit


Approach 1: Bit Shift

Approach 2: Brian Kernighan’s Algorithm (turn off the rightmost bit of one in a number)
